message:
fix(id): harden collision fallback and simplify issue_id_separator

Collision fallback:
- The desperate fallback at nonce > 1000 now re-checks existence
  before returning. Previously it returned the nonce-appended ID
  without verifying uniqueness, which could silently create a
  duplicate if the desperate ID already existed.
- Add a hard stop at nonce > 2000 that appends "-FINAL" to
  guarantee termination even if the exists() callback is broken
  (e.g., always returns true). This prevents an infinite loop in
  pathological scenarios.

issue_id_separator simplification:
- Replace the multi-step heuristic (is_numeric_segment,
  is_likely_hash_segment, fallback) with a simple rfind('-').
  The previous logic tried to distinguish hash segments from
  word segments but ultimately fell through to the rfind('-')
  fallback in all edge cases anyway, making the intermediate
  checks dead code.
- Remove the now-unused is_numeric_segment() and
  is_likely_hash_segment() helper functions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
